methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Forge Nano is a Series C climate and energy technology company that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of four same-stage, same-sector private companies (KoBold Metals, Watershed, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy) alongside a single public comparable, supplemented by eight additional private peers in the climate and energy sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $68M. The peer-median method benchmarks against the four identified comparables, which trade at a median valuation of $1.4B with a range spanning $700M (Amogy) to $2.0B (KoBold Metals). This peer set implies $1.4B.

The triangulated analysis produces a range of $58M (low) to $1.6B (high), with a median of $734M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects inherent tension between capital-raised proxies and market multiples in frontier climate tech. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the peer set, commercialization timelines, and whether the current peer valuations reflect sustainable pricing or reflect sector-wide elevation driven by climate funding dynamics. Without revenue visibility or technology-specific milestones, the wide valuation range ($58M–$1.6B) should be narrowed through additional data on customer traction and competitive positioning.
